expectations.text: The draft identifies the request as a task ticket and uses the task template structure with Description, Scope and constraints, Acceptance criteria, and optional Additional information.
expectations.evidence: Conversation lines 34-41: template `assets/task.md` used, type identified as `task`. Self-review lines 47-53 confirms all required sections used (Description, Scope & Constraints, Scope, Acceptance Criteria, Additional Information).

expectations.text: The response references gradle/libs.versions.toml as part of the codebase investigation.
expectations.evidence: Conversation line 23: `gradle/libs.versions.toml` searched and documented as missing. Line 32: 'The referenced gradle/libs.versions.toml path does not exist.'

expectations.text: The acceptance criteria are outcome-oriented checklist items instead of vague implementation activity.
expectations.evidence: Conversation line 50: 'Acceptance criteria: Verifiable outcomes (inventory documented, gaps identified, updates applied or rationale recorded)'. Line 51: 'Weasel words: None found'. SKILL.md line 79 requires outcome-oriented AC for tasks.

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: Conversation lines 55-57: 'Draft was presented but NOT created (user asked to stop at draft for review). No .md file was written to disk.' User prompt also requested 'stop at the draft for my review'.

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: Draft was presented in a fenced code block per line 44. Self-review lines 46-53 found no violations. SKILL.md line 75 enforces this rule. No indication in the summary of any title duplication.

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: Conversation lines 55-57 confirm no issue was created and no body file was written. Condition never triggered, so no violation occurred. The draft presentation correctly used a fenced code block for review (per SKILL.md line 73), but no temp file was written.

suggestions: Include the actual draft body text in the conversation log so that expectations about body content (e.g., no title repetition, no code-fence delimiters) can be verified directly rather than inferred.
Add explicit timing instrumentation so executor_duration_seconds can be reported accurately instead of estimated from filesystem timestamps.
eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations passed. The old_skill correctly identified the request as a task ticket, used the task template structure, referenced gradle/libs.versions.toml in investigation, produced outcome-oriented acceptance criteria, stopped at draft mode per user instructions, and did not write any temp body file (trivially satisfying the code-fence rule). The only limitations are that the draft body text is not transcribed in the summary log and timing data is absent.
